title: Level 154.1
wikitext:
{{CSS|User:Pexy0/monovoided.css}}__NOTOC__ {{Blurbackground|file=hm.jpg}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 154.1: '''"Dilapidated Platform"'''}}
'''“'''

For one who was curious enough to wander further through such an abandoned miracle would have seen how deep '''The Neon Express''' truly goes. Still the glimpse of what was left behind, but the sense of anxiety and urgency washes over you. May God bless those who end up in this inverted world.

''- Unknown''

[[File:train station-monochrome.png|center|700px|link=]] Just another day at [[Level 154|this aesthetic, articulately designed train station]]. The [[Architect Industries]] surely did one hell of a job in renovating '''The Neon Station''' into one of the main destinations for facilitating level transportation. As you traverse through the hallways, a certain thought arises in your mind: "Is it really worth staying here for long just to go through the same, monotonous work schedule every day?". Following a long period of self-doubt, you had decided, "You know what? I'm going to do my own thing. For an adventurous person like me, doing the same thing all over again halts my overall strength and prevents me from doing anything beyond conventional norms. Some minutes after, you come across a wooden door engulfed in thick, black smoke. Seeing the label on the door, you fearlessly declared, "What is this nonsense? 'Condemned'? Whoever came up with this name should be placed on psychiatric hold." While holding your breath, you slowly opened the door and then got sucked into another area. "That hurts..." you groaned in pain but shrugged it off soon after. You got on your feet and wondered, "Isn't this the same fuckin' thing but monochrome?" It really is, in a way; it's just because you weren't prepared for what was to come due to your extreme pride and ego. Going deeper inside, you found the same things around, but why does it look so oddly "abandoned"? The ceiling had broken apart, with various holes across the entire area, and the same vibe can't really be felt anymore in a place like this. Your curiosity still called you forward, though, and as always, you listened to it. You would end up regretting that very own decision soon after. ...
The minds of men are easy to break.
{{Class Custom Image|bg-image=Your Demise.webp|hexagon=3|class=3|safety=Unsafe|security=Deteriorating Security|entity=Mild Entity Count|text-color=#FFFFFF}} '''Level 154.1''', named "Dilapidated Platform," is a rare second-order sublevel to [[The Metro]] due to its parent level being [[Level 154]]. {{Blurimage|file=Frre.png|caption=An image of Level 154.1}} ==Description== '''Level 154.1''' is an enclosed, infinitely long, 2- to 16-meter-wide and consistently 2-meter-tall train station comprising a half-meter platform (present in 2-4 meter room variations) or 2 meters (present in 4-16 meter room variations). At the bare minimum, there are 2 concrete platforms present, and at most there are 4 present. The design of '''Level 154.1''' varies subtly, with pillars, different wall materials (brick or drywall), and light rows shifting positions. Each platform is interconnected by a straight concrete bridge. The sublevel is notable for having an effect that causes everything to be monochrome and more dim. The layout of the level is straightforward; a majority of the level is composed of the train station, but each platform in contact with each end of the walls has a short interconnecting passage to another platform, named ‘transition tunnels.’ Additionally, rare long hallways, also known as ‘accessibility tunnels,’ which link to more platforms and small rooms, can be present. Some platforms in the sublevel may appear to have a visible exit with escalators or stairs leading to the parent level, but these exits are not real and are blocked off by invisible barriers. Do not interact with the rails present in-between platforms, as they can fatally electrocute you, commonly killing wanderers. Instead, if one is to cross, one should check for any short concrete walkways to the other platform, which can be found eventually if you keep walking along. {{Blurimage|file=tunnels-monochrome.png|caption=Rare accessibility tunnels of Level 154.1|align=left}} Transition tunnels are typically 5 to 20 meters long, taking the appearance of a cylindrical concrete tunnel typically curving left and right sporadically. Lights line the ceiling, and small pipes and wires line the sides and the top. Do not stay near exposed pipes, as they have been found to flow a numerous amount of gases (albeit in small quantities). So far, groups have identified nitrogen (asphyxiation risk), carbon dioxide (suffocation risk), and helium (oxygen displacement), which are all involved with risks when in high quantities. Accessibility tunnels are long hallways (length undetermined), taking the appearance of a tall rectangular hallway with windows lining a random side. The hallway is known for accessibility to other parts of the level (hence the name), containing a numerous amount of doors on either side. The doors present on the side of the windows will be locked, and the ones on the other side either lead to other platforms or to other empty, miscellaneous small rooms with little to no items. Do not stay within these rooms for long, as the door could lock itself for a temporary amount of time (3 days~?). Once you enter a door leading to another platform, the door will disappear. The only way to find accessibility tunnels is merely luck. Despite '''Level 154.1''' having railways between platforms, a train rarely arrives. Common encounters often describe the train accelerating through the station. Being fast enough to carry small loose items or debris through, hurting nearby wanderers. Though, in cases it does stop for the station, the interior of the train appears to lead to [[The Void]] excluding the cab (the front of the train). Concrete walkways, which are used to travel to the other platform, are destroyed but manifest back after a week. {{Blurimage|file=Nopal.jpg|caption=Short transition tunnels to other platforms.}} The climate of '''Level 154.1''' is variable; temperatures instantly switching for 15 to 34 degrees Celsius randomly. Most of the time, the sublevel consists of no relative humidity, and rarely goes up to 25%. Additionally, the sublevel affects the following: liquids, unconditionally only able to evaporate faster, and metal, able to absorb and retain heat for prolonged periods. '''Level 154.1''', while appearing sterile at first, is found to be increasingly dirty in some areas; mold infestations along with water damage, causing indented or damaged layouts, and contamination found in tunnels (rubbish bags, damaged pipes). Despite the amount of water causing such damage, groups cannot determine the source. Some groups have taken the risk to enter through damaged parts of the ceiling to find a source and, secondly, exits or different areas. What was found is an infinite flat area with a low ceiling that has small holes leaving sunlight. Though, initial explorations have led to danger due to the lack of oxygen and due to the effects of the sunlight, which will be explained in the next paragraph. While sunlight is rare, when you encounter a direct source of it; stay away from it. Direct sunlight can cause first burns relatively quickly (0.5~ seconds) and 40 seconds of exposure can easily kill you. Direct sources like breaking a window in accessibility tunnels; though the window gives you adequate protection. The crawl space can also have small spots of sunlight; and those spots can get larger. Though; the windows can regenerate its material in a week. ==Entities== ===[[Entity 8|Hounds]]=== Hounds usually aren't as aggressive as they are in regular levels, though be aware that any loud noises or sudden movement can tip them off. The best way to stay safe is by bringing some fresh meat and feeding them whenever one attempts to approach a wanderer. This should give someone the time needed to flee from a Hound's line of sight. They usually reside within the hallways of '''Level 154.1''' which is something travelers should look out for. ===Amedeos=== {{Blurimage|file=trustworthy?.png|caption= Reported instance of an Amedeo.}} '''Amedeos''' are considered the main threat of '''Dilapidated Platform'''. They take on the appearance of shadowy figures, usually around the age of teenagers, gender varied. '''Amedeos''' would try to engage in a friendly conversation with lost and disoriented wanderers to the point when they forgot about their impending doom, with offers of assistance or armaments and supplies, yet, should one happen to look away from '''Amedeos''', they will find any means necessary to strangle them to death or push them off the train tracks. It is heavily advised to evade these entities instead of trying to fight back, as an '''Amedeo''''s strength slightly outshines that of an adult male. Items can also be used to distract them, giving one the opportunity to sprint from the danger zone. ==Colonies & Outposts== Establishing a residence in '''Level 154.1''' is theoretically possible thanks to useful items scattered across the level; however, the hostile conditions prove this detrimental in the long term. ==Theories== Many theories have surged surrounding the existence of '''Level 154.1''' in which two of the main ones have been listed here: ===1. The Karma Theory=== Some individuals believe that '''Level 154.1''' was created in order to punish those who were sinful in [[The Backrooms]]. This perhaps explains why the door to '''Dilapidated Platform''' from [[Level 154]] had a note with "CONDEMNED" written on it. Nevertheless, there is barely any concrete evidence to back it up, as most of these claims are made by members from religious groups, which may reduce the credibility of this case. ===2. [[Incident 154: Part I|The]] [[Incident 154: Part II|154]] [[Incident 154: Epilogue|Theory]]=== On the 23rd of December 2022, the Republic of Aurielle launched an all-out assault on [[Level 154]] in an attempt to retrieve as many Hermes Drive models as possible; however, while busy fighting with other factions within '''The Neon Station''', they inadvertently allowed the M.E.G. to seize the Model V Hermes Drive. The situation would eventually escalate into a war, with both sides throwing whatever they have at each other, which presumably caused some sections of '''The Neon Station''' to split apart and become their own level, hence the visible damage seen in '''Dilapidated Platform'''. 4 [[Architect Industries]] members would also testify towards this theory, as while cleaning up the debris left in [[Level 154]], they no-clipped into '''Level 154.1''' without any indication whatsoever. ==Public Documents== Several pieces of paper found referencing the 154 Incident were discovered in '''Level 154.1''', assumedly when parts of the level collapsed during [[Republic of Aurielle|Aurielle]] clashes with the [[M.E.G.]]. Due to the main page being locked down to prevent the spread of propaganda, they would be documented here instead. ===1. [[The Kauer Research Organization|K.R.O]]===
'''''(12/22/2022)''''' "To those who are on active duty in '''Level 154''', we request all units to swiftly evacuate to other K.R.O bases immediately. As the Republic of Aurielle's actions become increasingly clear each time, law and order cannot be maintained without the risk of an imminent attack. Their men has been mistreating Faceling staff members for days which is completely unacceptable. They come in there, they kill and murder, they pillage and destroy. We are doing our best to ensure the safety of all our friends and neighbors within '''The Neon Station''', but based on the atrocious and barbaric actions of them, our efforts are rendered infeasible, at least for now. Please inform everyone to move to safety as soon as you can. Leave behind any unnecessary belongings and do not engage with any hostile Aurielle soldiers. Thank you." - '''David Thorne | Chief of the Department of Public Affairs'''
'''''(12/15/2022)''''' "We have started to notice suspicious activities among Aurielle members within the region. Several cases of looting, pickpocketing, property destruction were reported in the last week alone. It is evident that they are searching for something vital to expand the group's influence. As tensions grow between the Republic of Aurielle and other settlements in '''Level 154''', it is encouraged to watch out for any further signs of aggression as to come up with an effective emergency response. We are currently trying to boost ties between the K.R.O and the M.E.G to prepare for these ordeals, even though it may cost us a lot in return. Agents will continue to be updated on the incident as soon as anything arrives on our doorstep. Thank you for your attention to this matter." - '''Alex I. Morgan | Head of Security'''
===2. [[M.E.G.]]===
Please remain calm as we are working on defusing the situation. We are well aware of the Aurielle's illicit activities in '''Level 154''' and are trying to come towards a diplomatic solution. A compromise will be needed for maintaining good faith between all outposts in '''The Neon Express'''. Nevertheless, were the Aurielle to refuse to back down, we would by all means necessary, intervene directly to prevent a bloodbath from happening. It appears that they're trying to collect a device called 'The Hermes Drives' which enables trains to function. Reaching it first should be a crucial step for stopping the Aurielle's malicious intents. A new type of weapon is also currently developed by our top researchers to arm ourselves better for cases like this in the future. We demand all stations to go to "Code Yellow" and brace themselves for the worst to happen. I'll make sure this sticks with you for the rest of your life, Jacet. - '''John H. Eckherman | Head of the M.E.G’s Department of Defense'''
===3. Recovered Logs===
'''Date: 12/23/2022'''
---- '''“The cold breeze flew into The Snack Stall’s premises as Faceling staff members were preparing to close down the store for Christmas. Most of them would usually be put on paid leave by now, but one stood out from the others. Nila, one of the workers, was one of the few people who excelled at their job of serving customers with striking speed and efficiency. When asked about what she does, she simply replied, “The work is taxing, sure, but my colleagues here make me feel whole. They treat me like a fellow member of the team, which is what I admire about them.” As she was packing stuff up, footsteps could be heard outside the hallways near where she was operating.”''' ---- '''Nila:''' Who are these people?
''[Radio noises could be heard in the meantime.]''
'''Auriellen Captain:''' On your orders, sir.
'''Command:''' Scout the area for any traces of the device; we will need it to enact our future plans. You are clear to engage any opposition forces. Do anything in your might to retrieve it back.
'''Auriellen Soldier 1:''' With pleasure, Commander.
'''Auriellen Soldier 2:''' Man, this doesn’t feel right. Don’t you think we’d better not risk another conflict with the meggies? Their heavy weaponry is enough to push us back in just a few weeks! Not to mention, one of our spies was captured and interrogated by them as well. They may as well have known of our plans from ages ago.
'''Auriellen Soldier 3:''' Are you going to stand up for what you believe in, or just whine about the potential outcomes of this? Grow up, man; you ain’t a 9-year-old anymore, dude.
'''Aurielle Captain:''' Cut that shit out. We move forward with the plan. 2, you search the trains. 1 and 3, inspect that dining area over there. Find out any clues that will lead us to finding the precious cargo.
'''Auriellen Soldier 1,2,3:''' Copy that.
''[Nila’s voice became trembling with intense fear.]''
'''Nila:''' I need to hide!
'''Auriellen Soldier 3:''' Who is that? Show yourself!
''[1 and 3 ferociously shot at Nila.]''
'''Auriellen Soldier 1:''' Stop right there! Where do you think you’re going? Shit! We lost her!
'''Nila:''' Christ, I’m hit; maybe this bandage should help out a bit for now. Hold on, why is my head spinning so much?
''[Nila peeked over the window just moments before falling unconscious.]''
'''Auriellen Captain:''' Burn everything. This place shall become a forgotten footnote of history.
'''Auriellen Soldiers:''' With pleasure, Captain.
''[Meanwhile, at M.E.G.'s Base Alpha...]''
'''Announcer:''' All stations, base security in '''Level 154''' has been compromised. Requesting immediate backup, over.
''[Rainy was snoring on one of the beds when someone woke her up.]''
'''Rainy:''' Claire!? What are you doing here? What time is it?
'''Claire:''' No matter, the situation is critical right now. Gear up and come with me to '''The Neon Station''', it's an entire fuckin' mess over there.
'''Rainy:''' Fine, at least you could have waken me up gently...
''[At The Hub's door to 154.]''
'''Rainy:''' Same shit different day eh? Did the Architects have their "pwecious" little Hermes Drives break like the shit it is again? Or is Base Omega pestering us about having to not hog all of the food from the Snack Stall?
'''Claire:''' Not really, we were alerted about a raid by the Auriellen Ground Forces in the level, and they are seemingly trying to steal the Hermes Drives within the level for themselves.
'''Rainy:''' Not these bastards again. They never know when to fuck off, don't they? Talk about being greedy...
''[Capt. Armand made his way through from the Metro.]''
'''Capt. Armand:''' Ughhh, this place stinks to high heaven. Whatever, don't worry boys, we'll have those damn auries cleaned by the end of the day.
'''M.E.G. Soldiers:''' Yes sir!
''[Armand ducked behind a piece of rubble and shot at a group of Auriellen soldiers.]''
'''Capt. Armand:''' Two down!
'''Auriellen Soldier 5:''' Fuck! I'm hit! Cover me!
'''Auriellen Soldier 7:''' It's either life or death now! I can't risk it.
'''Auriellen Soldier 5:''' I hope you burn in hell, selfish little prick.
'''Capt. Armand:''' Fire at will!
'''Auriellen Captain:''' We're taking fire from all directions! Command, requesting air support, over!
''[Rainy and Claire arrived just in time to see the bloodshed left behind.]''
'''Rainy:''' They certainly made a mess here. Okay. Since you're hard to notice and all, you go find and secure the Hermes Drive, and I'll go help the others out a bit.
''[Claire ran through the destroyed subway as Rainy stayed behind to confront the soldiers.]''
'''Rainy:''' Gotcha little guy!
'''Auriellen Soldier 6:''' Get your hands off me, gringo. I'll make sure your death is slow and painful!
'''Rainy:''' Not today, little mouse.
''[The soldier made one last effort at shooting Rainy in the face but failed miserably.]''
'''Rainy:''' So long.
''[Rainy pulled out a knife and stabbed him in the neck, watching as he bled out.]''
'''Rainy:''' Maybe pick your targets wisely next time in the afterlife, kid.
''[Back to Claire, she frantically searched for any signs of the Hermes Drives. Eventually she found a female Faceling holding what appeared to be a glass bottle containing exactly what they needed.]''
'''Nila:''' Hey. Name's Nila. What's your name.''
'''Rainy (mind):''' What an odd time to be asking this question...
'''Rainy:''' (sighs) My name's Claire. I'm a part of the Major Explorer Group. We need that Hermes Drives to keep it safe from the Auriellen soldiers that are invading the level right now.
''[On the battlefield, Anne, an operative for the Auriellen General Aerial Operations Command, had been tasked with bombing the meggies from above.]''
'''Anne (mind):''' These bombs are sure strong as hell but the drone itself is so fuckin' slow. Almost got myself killed because of this shit. I swear to god when I get back, I won't be so nice to the damned designers.
''[Anne precisely striked 2 APCs and destroyed them.]''
'''Anne:''' I didn't remember these meggies having such armored vehicles in the last battle. What the heck.
''[As she was talking, a bullet grazed her in the back.]''
'''Anne:''' Fucking savages!
'''Auriellen Soldier 1:''' Get her outta there. The rest of you, stay behind me.
''[Anne crawled to the designator and zoned in on an area through the cracked screen.]''
'''Anne:''' Have fun dodging this meggies.
''[Back at The Snack Stall, Nila and Claire were negotiating over the Hermes Drive. After a long and thorough discussion, Nila agreed to hand it over to Claire. As she was about to leave, she heard Nila's voice from behind.]''
'''Nila:''' Good luck, Claire.
'''Claire:''' You too, Nila.
'''[End of log]'''
'''Date: 1/5/2023'''
---- '''"Well, our ancestors weren’t wrong at all. History is written by the victors; history is filled with liars. Even when the M.E.G. succeeded in propelling Auriellen’s vicious attacks, it is unclear what they had in mind for the Hermes Drives. The future remains uncertain for all of us. Stay vigilant; the one you trust most may be the one who hurts you."''' ---- '''Facility P.A. Voice:''' Today, we declare victory against evil. Those auries thought colonising levels would be an easy and flawless objective, but little did they know it turned out to be one of their biggest strategic mistakes. I call on any remaining auries in '''Level 154''' to leave right away. You don't deserve to be here after all the horrific crimes your soldiers committed. I also would like to take the opportunity to honor our brave soldiers and friends who stood up against tyranny and those who had fallen to defend '''The Neon Station'''. May their sacrifices not be in vain.
''[As his voice vibrated through the now replaced speakers of '''Level 154'''. A team of 4 Architect Industries members were called in to clean things up.]''
'''Technician 2:''' What a rough day it is gonna be.
'''Technician 4:''' Can't believe they fought over the Hermes Drives, seems like a stupid reason to me. Were they setting up some kind of cinematic film for everyone to watch? As if the war itself made them look strong or anything.
'''Technician 1:''' Better not underestimate the power of it buddy, whoever gets their hands on that can do unprecedented things. As of now, we still haven't got it back yet. Are the meggies that scummy eh?
'''Technician 3:''' That's not the main problem right now. We gotta clean this mess up. Ridiculuous enough, they didn't dispatch a whole team or so to help us, instead opting to send merely the four of us in. I guess we're just unlucky.
'''Technician 2:''' I heard they were gonna deploy 30 more people here soon so let's just start with what we have right now.
'''Technician 4:''' Alright then, you two go check the wrecked trains over there and report any damages to Lockehart. Me and Josh will see if there are any supplies left in the Snack Stall.
''[The floor started creaking.]''
'''Technician 1:''' What the hell is wrong with this place now?
'''Technician 3:''' Hard to figure it out heh? Hey, how about you two come over here and check this out first?
'''Technician 2&4:''' If you say so.
''[They walked around for a while until the whole area began shaking.]''
'''Technician 2:''' WHAT'S GOING ON?
''[The floor collapsed soon after, sending all 4 technicians down to '''Level 154.1''']''
'''Technician 4:''' Bloody fuckin' hell. Maybe you shouldn't have told us to come over?
'''Technician 2:''' Yeah, are you two serious right now?
''[Just as Technician 2 was about to punch Technician 3 in the face.]''
'''Technician 1:''' Calm down Josh, blaming each other ain't gonna get us outta here.
'''Technician 2:''' Fine... I guess you're right.
'''Technician 4:''' Why does this place look so similar to where we were in? It's basically the same but in black and white.
'''Technician 3:''' Have no idea either man, let's split up and see if there is any escape route nearby.
''[Transmission was abruptedly cut.]
'''[End of log]'''
'''Date: 12/29/2022'''
---- '''Archiver Note:''' This log is a transcript of the first encounter of Amedeos. Encountered by a group of 3 young adults, named Johnny, Trey, and Dan, they uploaded a video recording to a BackChat forum. ---- '''Dan:''' Seen this before?
'''Johnny:''' I think we’re first dibs on this thing.
'''Trey:''' Wanna name the level then?
'''Johnny:''' I don’t think we’re that quick actu-
'''Johhny:''' What I meant is one of the first people.
'''Trey:''' Yea. Sure the megs’ got to this already.
'''Dan:''' God this monochrome filter is annoying. Doesn’t help with the lighting issues in this tunnel.
'''Trey:''' Hey while we’re here should we just start documenting?
'''Johnny:''' That would be silly. We would be credited.
'''Johnny:''' You don’t like boring…?
'''Dan:''' Stop.
'''Johnny:''' What the hell is that?
'''Dan:''' Probably like a face-
'''Amedeo:''' Hello? Yo.
'''Dan:''' That ain’t a Faceling.
'''Trey:''' Probably a fals-
'''Amedeo:''' I’m just normal seriously. Peace.
'''Trey:''' Wow I didn’t know people got to this level this fast. You lost?
'''Amedeo:''' You look more lost. I’m not, I actually belong to this huge group.
'''Dan:''' What.
'''Amedeo:''' Yea. I know right, we’re fast. Well, we transferred from Level 1. I think there’s around 63 of us? You can join us if you like.
'''Dan:''' Ah, no thanks.
'''Trey:''' We’re good.
'''Amedeo:''' Uh… well you guys look a bit lacking. Even though you don’t want to join us I can’t let a wanderer wander in pain. I have a few stacks of Almond Water in my bag. I can lend some if you want.
'''Johnny:''' Not traumatised yet huh? Well; for future trades please be more careful to people who trade to. Like us; you just met us like 2 minutes ago.
'''Amedeo:''' It’s fine if you don’t trust me I mean; I insist. Have some.
'''Trey:''' We are lacking a bit in some Almond’s, Dan.
'''Dan:''' Dang really?
'''Trey:''' We won’t last the next level.
'''Amedeo:''' So is that a yes…?
''[Dan turned around, checked for any doors before getting all of a sudden kicked by the Amedeo, sending him down to the tracks.]''
'''Trey:''' Holy shit.
'''Dan:''' WHAT THE FUCK ARE YOU D-
''[Dan was electrocuted by the rails before he could finish.]''
'''Johhny:''' Stop recording Trey. JUST START RUNNING!
'''[End of log]'''
== Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === *Entering a damaged grey door reading “do not open” in [[Level 154]] and [[Level 153]] will send one to this level. *Having a rating of -31 to -40 karma and riding a rollercoaster in [[Level 995]] has a chance to lead one here. * In far areas of [[Level 154]], more specifically areas former for their conflict in the war can have a high chance of no-clipping you here. * There is a small chance when you get electrocuted (then as a result pass out), you will end up here. * Travelling in the most barren parts of [[Level 28]] can transition the level into Level 154.1’s crawlspace. === Exits === * Going into a train (the void part) will most of the time, send you into [[The Void]]. You may have a small chance of ending up in a dark part of [[Level 66]]. * Killing an Amedeo sends you into [[The Hive]], although this remains rumoured for obvious reasons. * The doors on the window side of accessibility tunnels have a chance of leading into [[Level 188]] or [[Level 5]]. * Finding a claustrophobic, mist covered part of the level sends you to [[Level 155]]. *Finding a ladder that goes down leads to either [[Level 154]], [[Level 164]], or rarely, [[Level 1]]. *Finding a grey door reading “ways out” will lead you back to [[Level 154]]. {{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author & Licensing Information'''|final='''> Close Author & Licensing Information'''|body= '''Original Authors''': [[User:IceCraft2022|IceCraft2022]] (Intro, Colonies & Outposts, Entities, Theories, Public Documents 1, 2, 3 (Log 1 & 2)), [[User:PlutoniumOnInternet|Plutonium]] (Description, Public Documents (Log 3), Entrances & Exits).
